---
title: "Single-Cell Omics Tutorials"
subtitle: "End-to-end workflows for scRNA-seq, scATAC-seq, Spatial Transcriptomics, Multi-omics, and Genotyping in R"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div style="
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #0A1018 0%, #0F2030 50%, #0D2A1E 100%);
  border: 1px solid #1E3040;
  border-radius: 12px;
  padding: 3.5rem 3rem;
  margin-bottom: 3rem;
  position: relative;
  overflow: hidden;
">
  <div style="
    position: absolute; top: -60px; right: -60px;
    width: 280px; height: 280px; border-radius: 50%;
    background: radial-gradient(circle, rgba(26,147,111,0.15) 0%, transparent 70%);
    pointer-events: none;
  "></div>

  <div style="font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.78rem;
    color: #88D498; letter-spacing: 0.12em; margin-bottom: 1rem;">
    R · SEURAT · ARCHR · SIGNAC · HARMONY · MONOCLE3 · COMPLEXHEATMAP
  </div>

  <h1 style="font-family: 'Syne', sans-serif; font-size: 3rem;
    color: #E8F4F0; font-weight: 800; line-height: 1.15; margin: 0 0 1.2rem 0;">
    Single-Cell<br>Omics Tutorials
  </h1>

  <p style="font-size: 1.05rem; color: #8FA8B8; max-width: 600px;
    line-height: 1.8; margin: 0 0 2rem 0;">
    Reproducible, well-documented analysis pipelines for researchers working with
    single-cell RNA-seq, ATAC-seq, spatial transcriptomics, multi-omics integration,
    and genotyping. Designed for experienced R users.
  </p>

  <div style="display: flex; gap: 1rem; flex-wrap: wrap;">
    <a href="getting-started.qmd" style="
      background: #1A936F; color: #E8F4F0;
      padding: 0.65rem 1.6rem; border-radius: 6px;
      font-family: 'DM Sans', sans-serif; font-weight: 500;
      text-decoration: none; font-size: 0.95rem;
      transition: background 0.2s;">
      Get Started →
    </a>
    <a href="https://github.com/tipsarin/scOmics" style="
      background: transparent; color: #88D498;
      padding: 0.65rem 1.6rem; border-radius: 6px;
      border: 1px solid #1A936F;
      font-family: 'DM Sans', sans-serif; font-weight: 500;
      text-decoration: none; font-size: 0.95rem;">
      GitHub
    </a>
  </div>
</div>
```

## What's Covered

This tutorial series walks through complete single-cell omics analysis workflows,
from raw data ingestion to publication-ready figures.

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

### 🧬 scRNA-seq
Using **Seurat v5** as the primary framework.

| Chapter | Topic |
|---|---|
| 01 | QC & Filtering |
| 02 | Normalization & HVG Selection |
| 03 | PCA · UMAP · tSNE |
| 04 | Graph-based Clustering |
| 05 | Cell Type Annotation |
| 06 | Differential Expression + ComplexHeatmap |
| 07 | Trajectory Analysis (Monocle3) |
| 08 | Batch Integration (Harmony) |

### 🔓 scATAC-seq
Using **ArchR** for project management and peak analysis.

| Chapter | Topic |
|---|---|
| 01 | QC & Arrow File Creation |
| 02 | LSI · UMAP |
| 03 | Peak Annotation & Gene Scores |
| 04 | TF Motif Analysis (chromVAR) |

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

### 🗺️ Spatial Transcriptomics
Using **Seurat** with 10x Visium data.

| Chapter | Topic |
|---|---|
| 01 | Loading & Spot-level QC |
| 02 | Spatially Variable Genes |
| 03 | Cell Type Deconvolution |

### 🔗 Multi-omics & Genotyping
Integration strategies and donor demultiplexing.

| Chapter | Topic |
|---|---|
| 01 | WNN Integration (RNA + ATAC) |
| 02 | Signac: Linked Peaks to Genes |
| 03 | Donor Demultiplexing |

:::

:::

---

## Software Versions

Tutorials are developed and tested with the following versions:

| Package | Version | Purpose |
|---------|---------|---------|
| R | ≥ 4.3.0 | Base environment |
| Seurat | 5.x | scRNA-seq, Spatial, WNN |
| ArchR | 1.0.3 | scATAC-seq |
| Signac | 1.13.0 | Chromatin accessibility |
| Monocle3 | 1.3.x | Trajectory analysis |
| Harmony | 1.2.x | Batch integration |
| ComplexHeatmap | 2.18.x | Visualization |
| ggplot2 | 3.5.x | Visualization |

: {tbl-colwidths="[25,20,55]"}

---

## How to Use These Tutorials

Each chapter follows a consistent structure:

1. **Overview** — biological rationale and goals
2. **Setup** — required packages and data
3. **Code walkthrough** — annotated, reproducible R code
4. **Parameter guidance** — when and how to adjust defaults
5. **Checkpoints** — `saveRDS()` calls to preserve progress
6. **Session info** — exact package versions used

::: {.callout-tip}
## Reproducibility
All tutorials use `execute: freeze: auto` in Quarto, meaning outputs are cached
after the first render. Enable this by committing the `_freeze/` directory to your repo.
:::
